task-1772536103557-4qcnmg.description:
Artifact Persistence & Cross-Session File Editing (Claude Web UI behavior)

Requirements:
1. FILES GENERATED IN SESSION ARE PERSISTENT - When a skill execution produces files (PDFs, images, code, data files), they must be stored persistently, not just in ephemeral sandbox storage.

2. IN-SESSION EDITING - Generated files (e.g. a PDF, a script, a dataset) should be editable within the same session. User can modify the file and re-run or download the updated version.

3. CROSS-SESSION PERSISTENCE - When a user logs out and logs back in, all previously generated files from past sessions must still be visible, accessible, and editable. Files are tied to user/tenant + execution history.

4. SESSION-FILE ASSOCIATION - Each file is linked to the session/execution that created it, so users can browse their history and find artifacts from past runs.

Implementation approach:
- Store generated artifacts in MinIO (S3) with metadata linking to tenant, session, execution
- Add a file management API: GET /files (list), GET /files/{id} (download), PUT /files/{id} (update/edit), DELETE /files/{id}
- Add session persistence: store session metadata in PostgreSQL with references to artifact paths in MinIO
- OpenSandbox integration: after skill execution, download artifacts from sandbox and persist to MinIO before destroying the sandbox
- Add file versioning: when a file is edited, store as a new version while keeping history
- Frontend contract: API returns file metadata (name, type, size, created_at, session_id, execution_id, versions) for UI to render like Claude web artifacts

This is a sub-task of the OpenSandbox integration — artifacts downloaded from OpenSandbox sandboxes must flow into this persistence layer.
